summary: Malaysia's post-quantum transition is led by CyberSecurity Malaysia, the national cyber security specialist agency, which has published a Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration Framework and a technical guide on adopting and implementing post-quantum cryptographic solutions, and offers migration consultancy, awareness and workshop services to government agencies, critical infrastructure operators and private organisations. This work sits alongside the national MyKriptografi and MySEAL cryptography programmes. The Ministry of Digital, working with the National Cyber Security Agency (NACSA) and the domain registry MYNIC, has publicly framed the move to post-quantum cryptography as an urgent necessity, including the need to replace at-risk protocols such as DNSSEC. The published material is guidance rather than binding law, and no dated national migration timeline is set out in the ingested documents.
govActors:
CyberSecurity Malaysia | national cyber security specialist agency, publishes the PQC Migration Framework, adoption recommendations and PQC migration services
NACSA | National Cyber Security Agency, sets national cyber security policy and participates in the post-quantum transition messaging
Ministry of Digital | government ministry framing the move to post-quantum cryptography as an urgent national necessity, including replacing at-risk DNSSEC protocols
MYNIC | national domain name registry, partner in the quantum-and-internet-security awareness programme
mainRegulation: CyberSecurity Malaysia Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration Framework | national | guidance
